original_text:
### Capitalization & Casing
- **Headlines/headings:** Initial caps (title case) — capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions. Example: *LISRAYA Targets the Root Cause of Dermatomyositis*.
- **Campaign headline "YOU DESERVE MORE": ALL CAPS, always** (see 1.6).
- **Chart/infographic headlines: ALL CAPS**, set in Agenda (print) / Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback).
- Body copy: sentence case.
- **[GENERAL]** Do not use ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheads other than the cases above.

relations.note: The prohibition on ALL CAPS establishes campaign headlines and charts as the exclusive exceptions.

quotes: Headlines/headings: Initial caps (title case) — capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions.

rules.intent: Establish standard editorial casing across brand headlines and body copy.
rules.rule_text: Headlines and headings use Initial Caps (title case) as defined by the standard headline casing token (case.general.headline = "title_case"), while standard body copy uses sentence case (case.general.body = "sentence_case").

quotes: Campaign headline "YOU DESERVE MORE": ALL CAPS, always
Campaign headline "YOU DESERVE MORE": ALL CAPS, always
Chart/infographic headlines: ALL CAPS, set in Agenda (print) / Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback).

rules.intent: Maintain graphic impact and consistency for key campaign and infographic copy elements.
rules.rule_text: Campaign headlines (specifically 'YOU DESERVE MORE') and chart/infographic headlines must always be set in all caps (case.campaign.headline = "all_caps"). Chart headlines are set in Agenda (or the fallback Nunito Sans Bold+).

quotes: Do not use ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheads other than the cases above.
Do not use ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheads other than the cases above.

rules.intent: Maintain optimal legibility and standard hierarchy by restricting ALL CAPS usage.
rules.rule_text: Do not use ALL CAPS styling for body copy, buttons, or subheads except for explicitly approved campaign and chart/infographic headline cases.
